subject: bowser listener
truth: Stop relying on the Bowser listener; it has been removed from Responder.
quote: removed bowser listener

subject: Machine account dump feature
truth: Stop relying on machine account dumps; they have been removed because they are not crackable.
quote: Removed machine accounts dump, since they are not crackable

subject: -r command-line option
truth: Stop using the -r flag; it has been removed and all references to it removed from help.
quote: removed -r reference from help msg.

subject: RespondTo/DontRespondTo behaviour
old: RespondTo/DontRespondTo had a bug causing incorrect behaviour
new: The RespondTo/DontRespondTo filtering now works correctly
truth: The RespondTo/DontRespondTo filtering logic was fixed; existing configurations that relied on the broken behaviour may now behave differently.
quote: fixed the RespondTo/DontRespondTo issue

from_fact: respondto/dontrespondto had a bug causing incorrect behaviour
the respondto/dontrespondto filtering now works correctly

subject: SMTP credential interception
old: SMTP credential interception had a soft failure causing missed captures
new: SMTP credential interception now correctly captures credentials that were previously missed
truth: SMTP credential interception was fixed; previously missed credentials will now be captured, which may change expected behaviour if you relied on the soft failure.
quote: Fixing soft failure which results in missed SMTP credential interception

from_fact: smtp credential interception had a soft failure causing missed captures
smtp credential interception now correctly captures credentials that were previously missed

subject: HTTP basic auth parsing with colons in password
old: HTTP basic auth parsing broke when password contained colons
new: HTTP basic auth parsing now correctly handles passwords containing colons
truth: HTTP basic auth parsing was fixed to handle passwords with colons; previously broken captures of such passwords will now succeed.
quote: fixed HTTP basic auth parsing when password contains colons

from_fact: http basic auth parsing broke when password contained colons
http basic auth parsing now correctly handles passwords containing colons

subject: Proxy-Auth potential disruption
old: Proxy-Auth had a potential disruption issue
truth: Proxy-Auth was fixed to prevent potential disruption; behaviour may change if you relied on the disrupted state.
quote: Fixed potential disruption on Proxy-Auth

from_fact: proxy-auth had a potential disruption issue

subject: fingerprint.py
truth: Stop using fingerprint.py; it has been removed from the repository.
quote: removed fingerprint.py

subject: DNS analyze mode
truth: Stop using analyze mode on DNS; it has been removed because ARP is required to get queries.
quote: Remove analyze mode on DNS since you need to ARP to get queries

subject: old DHCP script
truth: Stop using the old standalone DHCP script; it has been replaced by the built-in DHCP Responder module.
quote: Removed old DHCP script since its now a Responder module.

subject: default certificates
old: static default certificates bundled with Responder
truth: Stop relying on static default certificates; they have been removed and certificates are now auto-generated on startup.
quote: removed default certs

from_fact: static default certificates bundled with responder

subject: static certificates
old: static certificates bundled with the tool
truth: Stop using the static bundled certificates; they have been removed and replaced with automatic certificate generation on each run.
quote: Removed the static certs and added automatic cert generation

from_fact: static certificates bundled with the tool

subject: FindSMB2UPTime.py script
truth: Stop using FindSMB2UPTime.py; RunFinger already provides this information.
quote: removed FindSMB2UPTime.py since RunFinger already get this info

subject: MultiRelay binaries
truth: Stop using MultiRelay binaries; they have been removed from the repository.
quote: Removed MultiRelay binaries

subject: BindShell executable file
truth: Stop using the BindShell executable; it has been removed from the repository.
quote: Removed BindShell executable file

subject: LDAP hash parsing algorithm
truth: The complete LDAP hash parsing algorithm was changed to fix an NTLMv2 bug; existing NTLMv2 hashes parsed from LDAP may no longer be extracted correctly.
quote: Changed the complete LDAP parsing hash algo (ntlmv2 bug).

subject: ThreadingMixIn
truth: Stop using ThreadingMixIn; MultiRelay now processes one request at a time and queues subsequent requests.
quote: Removed ThreadingMixIn. MultiRelay should process one request at the timeand queue the next ones.

subject: SMB support
truth: SMBv2 is now enabled by default; existing setups that relied on SMBv1-only behaviour may now negotiate SMBv2.
quote: Added SMBv2 support enabled by default.

subject: IsOSX function
truth: Replace calls to IsOSX with utils.IsOsX.
quote: change IsOSX to utils.IsOsX. Fixes #89

subject: config dump location
new: config dump in a new separate log file
truth: The config dump is no longer written to Responder-Session.log; a new separate log file is created with host network config and config dump.
quote: Removed the config dump in Responder-Session.log. New file gets created in logs, with host network config such as dns, routes, ifconfig and config dump

from_fact: config dump in a new separate log file

subject: old style command-line options
truth: Stop using old style options like '-r On'; use '-r' directly instead.
quote: Removed: old style options. Just use -r instead of -r On

subject: Responder verbosity
new: Responder is now less verbose by default; use -v for verbose mode
truth: Responder is now less verbose by default; you must pass the -v command switch to enable verbose output.
quote: added: Command switch -v for verbose mode. Responder is now less verbose.

from_fact: responder is now less verbose by default; use -v for verbose mode

subject: CLI options replaced by configuration file
old: Several CLI options were available directly on the command line
new: Those CLI options have been removed and replaced by a configuration file (Responder.conf)
truth: Several CLI options have been removed and replaced by settings in the Responder.conf configuration file.
quote: Added: Configuration file, removed several cli options and several fixes.

from_fact: several cli options were available directly on the command line
those cli options have been removed and replaced by a configuration file (responder.conf)

subject: WPAD default setting
truth: WPAD is now disabled by default; use -w On to enable it.
quote: Changed WPAD to Off by default. Use command line -w On to enable.

subject: Output file extension
truth: Output files no longer have a .txt extension; they are saved with no extension.
quote: changed .txt to no extension.

subject: Bind to interface support on macOS (OsX)
old: Could bind to a specific interface on OsX
truth: Bind to interface support is removed for OsX; Responder on OsX can only listen on all interfaces.
quote: Removed bind to interface support for OsX. Responder for OsX can only listen on all interfaces.

from_fact: could bind to a specific interface on osx

subject: SRV answer additional RRs
old: Responder included additional resource records in SRV answers
new: Responder no longer includes additional RRs in SRV answers
truth: SRV answers no longer include additional resource records; any code or tooling that parsed those extra RRs will break.
quote: removed addiontional RR on SRV answers

from_fact: responder included additional resource records in srv answers
responder no longer includes additional rrs in srv answers
